description: Tasks rewritten to match actual generated data

tasks.instruction: Select and begin configuring the cheapest virtual machine in the US-East region that has at least 4 vCPUs, at least 16 GB of memory, and costs no more than $120 per month, using a current Linux LTS operating system and a descriptive research-related instance name.

tasks.instruction: Create an auto-scaling group in the EU-West region for a web service using a general-purpose VM type with 2 vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, set its minimum capacity to 2 and maximum to 5 instances, scale out when CPU utilization exceeds 70%, and save the configuration.

tasks.instruction: Configure an alert for any production-tagged service so that it triggers with High severity when average CPU utilization is greater than 80% for at least 10 consecutive minutes, then save it.

tasks.instruction: Create a new monitoring dashboard summarizing health for a single API-type service by adding three time-series widgets for average CPU utilization, HTTP 5xx error rate, and p95 latency over the last 24 hours, arranging CPU on top and the other two below, and save the dashboard.

tasks.instruction: From the managed database plans, select the cheapest option in any region that has at least 2 vCPUs, at least 100 GB of storage, includes automatic daily backups, and costs no more than $200 per month, then add it to a comparison or favorites list.

tasks.instruction: For an existing virtual machine whose name contains the word 'app', configure a daily backup policy scheduled at 02:00 in its local timezone that retains backups for 30 days and apply it to that VM.

tasks.instruction: Using the pricing calculator, build a monthly estimate in a single region that includes 3 general-purpose virtual machines, 1 production-suitable managed database, and 1 load balancer, adjusting sizes so that the total estimated monthly cost is under $500, then save the estimate.

tasks.instruction: Enable AI-based anomaly detection for a log stream related to payments, using a 14-day training window with high sensitivity and focusing on an appropriate error or latency field, then save the rule.

tasks.instruction: Create an incident automation rule that monitors HTTP 5xx error rate across all services and, when it exceeds 5% for 5 consecutive minutes, opens a High-priority incident assigned to the reliability-focused team, then save the rule.
